summary: Matched `src/melee/it/items/itpeachtoad.c` 100%, replacing placeholder nonmatching markers with item state table setup, Peach/Kirby owner callbacks, spawn/setup logic, animation-state callbacks, and corrected function prototypes. The build configuration was updated to classify `melee/it/items/itpeachtoad.c` as `Matching` instead of `NonMatching`.
what_changed: `itpeachtoad.c` grew from placeholder `/// #symbol` stubs into matched C implementations for `it_802BDE18`, `it_802BDEFC`, `it_802BDF40`, `it_802BDFA0`, `it_802BDFC0`, `it_802BDFE0`, `it_802BE02C`, `it_802BE100`, `it_802BE168`, and the wrapper `it_802BE1F4`.
Added `ItemStateTable it_803F7528[]` with two states: state `0` calling `it_802BE02C` and state `1` calling `it_802BE168`.

Added Peach/Kirby branch helpers: `itpeachtoad_inline_1` calls either `ftPe_SpecialN_DoDeath2` or `ftKb_SpecialNPe_8010C3C0` depending on `item->kind`; `itpeachtoad_inline_2` checks active state with either `ftPe_SpecialN_IsActive` or `ftKb_SpecialNPe_8010C4AC`, returning true when there is no owner.

Implemented Toad item spawning in `it_802BDE18` using a local `SpawnItem`, setting kind, positions, facing direction, parent GObj fields, zero velocity/damage, and flag `x44_flag.b0`, then calling `Item_80268B18`, `Item_8026AB54`, and `db_80225DD8` on success.

Implemented animation and item lifecycle callbacks using item helpers including `Item_80268E5C`, `Item_802694CC`, `it_8026BB44`, `it_80274574`, `it_8026BB20`, `it_8026B724`, `it_8026B73C`, and `it_8026B894`.
Added frame-threshold behavior: `it_802BE02C` triggers `it_8026BB20` at animation frame `7.0f` and `it_8026BB44` at `>= 53.0f`; `it_802BE168` calls `it_8026BB44` at `>= 60.0f`.

`it_802BE100` manipulates an `HSD_JObj` from `item_gobj->hsd_obj`, calls `Item_80268E5C` for state `1`, temporarily applies `lb_8000BA0C(hsd_jobj, 10.0f)`, runs `HSD_JObjAnimAll`, restores with `lb_8000BA0C(hsd_jobj, 1.0f)`, then calls `it_80274574`.

`itpeachtoad.h` tightened prototypes: `it_802BDE18` now returns `Item_GObj*` and takes `ItemKind`/`f32`; `it_802BE02C` and `it_802BE168` now return `bool` and take `Item_GObj*` instead of `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS`.
smart_moves: Used small static inline helpers to share the owner-notification and owner-active checks between Peach's native Toad item and Kirby's copied Peach Neutral-B path, reducing duplication while preserving the `item->kind` branch behavior needed for matching.

Kept stack-shaping padding locals such as `u8 _[8];` in functions like `it_802BDFE0` and `it_802BE100`, which is a common decomp matching tactic when the source logic is simple but stack layout matters.
Re-read `GET_ITEM(item_gobj)` before later checks/calls in `it_802BE02C` and `it_802BE168`, suggesting attention to compiler output and aliasing/codegen rather than aggressively simplifying the C.

Updated the header at the same time as the implementation, replacing unknown signatures with concrete `Item_GObj*`, `ItemKind`, `f32`, and `bool` types so downstream files can use stronger declarations.
Moved the file to `Matching` in `configure.py` only after supplying the full matched implementation.
decomp_lessons: When matching item files, complete the full loop: implement the C, expose accurate prototypes in the header, define the item state table, and flip the object from `NonMatching` to `Matching` in `configure.py`.
Peach/Kirby shared special-move items may need runtime `item->kind` branches to call either the original fighter-specific function or the Kirby-copy equivalent.
For item spawn functions, preserve detailed `SpawnItem` initialization order and fields, including duplicated parent GObj fields and bit flags, because these can affect matching.
Animation callbacks may require exact floating constants such as `7.0f`, `53.0f`, and `60.0f` and exact helper-call ordering.
Temporary animation-rate or joint-scale manipulation around `HSD_JObjAnimAll` can be represented with calls like `lb_8000BA0C(jobj, 10.0f)` followed by restore to `1.0f`.
Do not over-clean matched code: extra local declarations, padding arrays, repeated `GET_ITEM` calls, and simple wrapper functions can be intentional for codegen.
naming_conventions: Reviewer feedback preferred the shorter local name `jobj` over a verbose `hsd_jobj` for an `HSD_JObj*` local.
The implementation used `item_gobj` for item object parameters, improving clarity over the previous `gobj` in `it_802BE1F4`.
Spawn-related locals followed clear roles: `spawn_gobj` for the returned item object and `spawn` for the `SpawnItem` initializer.

Static inline helper names remained provisional/address-file scoped (`itpeachtoad_inline_1`, `itpeachtoad_inline_2`), with no evidence in the PR excerpt of semantic renaming beyond reviewer advice for `jobj`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use `static inline` helpers when repeated logic must appear in multiple functions but still match expected inlining/codegen.
Introduce unused padding locals such as `u8 _[8];` to match stack frame layout.
Preserve repeated loads like repeated `GET_ITEM(item_gobj)` rather than caching across all logic if the target assembly reloads.
Preserve exact float suffixes and constants (`0.0F`, `7.0f`, `10.0f`, `1.0f`, `53.0f`, `60.0f`).
Use concrete enum/typedef signatures (`ItemKind`, `f32`, `bool`) once identified, replacing `enum_t`, `float`, `UNK_RET`, and `UNK_PARAMS`.
Keep wrapper functions around one-line item helpers when they correspond to separate symbols, e.g. `it_802BDFA0`, `it_802BDFC0`, and `it_802BE1F4`.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.lesson: For `HSD_JObj*` locals, concise `jobj` naming is preferred over `hsd_jobj` when there is no ambiguity.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.comment: Nice work, thanks for the contributions.
review_feedback.lesson: Overall reviewer tone was positive; no substantive matching objections are visible in the provided excerpt.

follow_up_queries: Find other Peach/Kirby copied-special item files that branch on `item->kind` between `ftPe_*` and `ftKb_*` functions.
Search for `u8 _[8];` in matched item files to compare stack-padding patterns.
Search for `Item_80268E5C` plus `ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE` to reuse item animation callback structure.
Compare `itpeachtoadspore.c`, `itpeachparasol.c`, and `itpeachturnip.c` for similar item state table and spawn conventions.
Check whether later PRs renamed `hsd_jobj` to `jobj` in `it_802BE100` following the review comment.
